item.title: Career Opportunity: Technical Programme Officer (P2) at the International Labour Organization in Bangkok 2026
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
Technical Programme Officer (P2) at the International Labour Organization in Bangkok 2026. The International Labour Organization, a specialized agency of the United Nations established in 1919, brings together governments, employers, and workers across 187 member states. Driven by a mission to establish international labour standards, develop progressive policies, and design programmes that foster social justice, […]

The post Career Opportunity: Technical Programme Officer (P2) at the International Labour Organization in Bangkok 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Technical Programme Officer (P2) at the International Labour Organization in Bangkok 2026.

The International Labour Organization, a specialized agency of the United Nations established in 1919, brings together governments, employers, and workers across 187 member states. Driven by a mission to establish international labour standards, develop progressive policies, and design programmes that foster social justice, the ILO remains at the forefront of economic and social progress.

Under the umbrella of the Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific, the ILO-Korea Partnership Programme supports crucial regional development initiatives. These projects align with the overarching ILO strategy, particularly the Global Accelerator for Just Transitions. A major focus of this programme is addressing youth employment by promoting decent jobs, strengthening local employment services, and equipping young people with modern digital and green skills.

To support these regional objectives, the ILO is seeking a qualified Technical Programme Officer for a P2-grade, short-term position based in Bangkok, Thailand. This role provides an exceptional opportunity to work alongside international development experts, key national partners, and donor institutions to drive impactful change across the Asia-Pacific region.

Core Information and Key Dates

Candidates interested in applying for this professional-level UN vacancy must take note of the administrative details outlined below:

  • Vacancy Number: DC/BANGKOK/P/2026/02
  • Job ID: 13728
  • Publication Date: 9 July 2026
  • Application Deadline: 23 July 2026 (Midnight, Bangkok Time)
  • Duty Station: Bangkok, Thailand
  • Department / Unit: Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific (RO-Asia and the Pacific)
  • Contract Type: Short-Term Technical Cooperation Appointment
  • Contract Duration: 5 Months

The Role of the Technical Programme Officer

The successful candidate will assist the Chief Technical Advisor based in Bangkok with the day-to-day management and execution of the ILO-Korea Partnership Programme. Operating within established ILO policies, the Technical Programme Officer provides direct technical and administrative support to meet the programme’s strategic targets. This role requires close collaboration with regional programme teams, ILO constituents, external development partners, headquarters technical units, and the Bangkok Decent Work Team.

Key Reporting Lines

The Technical Programme Officer reports directly to the Chief Technical Advisor of the ILO-Korea Partnership Programme. Additional technical guidance is provided by specialists in the ILO Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific, the Bangkok Decent Work Team, and dedicated departments at ILO Headquarters in Geneva.

Description of Duties and Responsibilities

Working under the supervision of the Chief Technical Advisor, the incumbent will be responsible for a wide array of technical and administrative tasks, categorized into key operational areas:

1. Project Management and Internal Collaboration

  • Assist in the monitoring and evaluation of project activities to ensure compliance with ILO operational guidelines.
  • Incorporate gender-responsive methodologies into project tracking and evaluation framework designs.
  • Collaborate with field offices, headquarters units, and active project networks to share technical inputs and scale up best practices.

2. Stakeholder and Donor Engagement

  • Support and maintain strong working partnerships with Korean partner institutions, handling necessary project planning, progress reporting, and public visibility requirements.
  • Strengthen institutional cooperation with government ministries, workers’ organizations, employers’ associations, UN partner agencies, and non-governmental organizations.

3. Event and Capacity Building Management

  • Plan, coordinate, and lead key programme events, such as regional conferences, technical capacity-building workshops, knowledge-sharing seminars, and high-level policy dialogues.
  • Ensure all scheduled events meet the strategic objectives of the programme and adhere to donor visibility protocols.

4. Technical Backstopping and Reporting

  • Provide robust administrative and technical backstopping to project implementation teams.
  • Draft and prepare comprehensive technical progress reports, identify lessons learned, and propose risk-mitigation measures for ongoing activities.
  • Ensure gender mainstreaming considerations are actively integrated into every stage of the project cycle.

5. Missions and General Representation

  • Undertake official project-related travel and technical missions within the region as required to advance project objectives.

Required Professional Qualifications and Competencies

To qualify for this P2-level international civil service position, candidates must meet specific criteria across education, work experience, languages, and technical competencies:

Educational Background

  • Minimum Requirement: A first-level university degree (Bachelor’s or equivalent) in economics, social policy, public finance, or another closely related academic field.
  • Preferred Qualification: An advanced university degree (Master’s or equivalent) focusing on employment, skills development, and social protection frameworks.

Professional Experience

  • At least three years of progressive professional experience in international development, specifically focusing on employment promotion and skills development.
  • Practical familiarity with the thematic areas of Just Transition, Social Protection, Skills Development, and Social Dialogue.
  • Proven experience in organizing and managing multi-stakeholder workshops, technical events, or conferences within an international development context.

Language Skills

  • An excellent command of written and spoken English is required.

Core Technical and Interpersonal Competencies

  1. Institutional Knowledge: Familiarity with the structures, policies, and procedures of the United Nations system, ministries of labour, or related governmental agencies is highly advantageous.
  2. Regional Context: Deep knowledge of development challenges within the Asia-Pacific region, specifically targeting conditions in Cambodia, Mongolia, the Philippines, Thailand, and Viet Nam.
  3. Project Management: Strong organizational skills to run multiple, complex, and concurrent project activities under tight deadlines.
  4. Analytical Capability: Ability to carry out comprehensive analyses of socio-economic factors and project requirements to guide strategic decisions.
  5. Communication: Exceptional written and oral communication skills, including the ability to draft clear project documents and liaise confidently with external donors.
  6. Teamwork and Empathy: Proven ability to work effectively in a multicultural environment, utilizing interpersonal skills to build trust, collaboration, and gender-sensitive dynamics.

Terms and Conditions of Employment

This technical cooperation position is filled through direct selection by the Director-General of the ILO. It is important to note the following conditions of employment:

  • Career Pathing: Technical cooperation appointments are short-term and are not expected to lead to a career in the ILO. They do not carry an expectation of contract renewal or conversion to other appointment types.
  • Contract Extensions: Any extension of this five-month contract is strictly dependent on the availability of funds, the continuing need of the technical functions, and satisfactory performance and conduct.
  • Medical Assessment: Any offer of employment is conditional upon the candidate receiving medical clearance from the ILO Medical Adviser, verifying physical fitness for the specific demands of the position.

How to Apply

All interested candidates must submit their applications online through the official recruitment portal.

  1. To review complete details and submit an application, please visit ILO Jobs.
  2. The application portal provides detailed, step-by-step instructions on completing the online profile.
  3. Only candidates selected for further assessment, such as a written examination or an online interview, will be contacted directly.
  4. Assessments may be conducted virtually using remote communication tools, including video conferencing, email assessments, or telephone interviews.

Conclusion

The Technical Programme Officer (P2) position with the ILO-Korea Partnership Programme in Bangkok is a valuable entry point for professionals dedicated to social justice, green transition, and youth development. It offers a structured environment to apply project management skills, collaborate with global stakeholders, and contribute to decent work strategies across Asia and the Pacific. Qualified candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications thoroughly and submit them before the deadline of 23 July 2026.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

The post Career Opportunity: Technical Programme Officer (P2) at the International Labour Organization in Bangkok 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Building Africa’s Next Generation of AI-Powered Female Innovators: Apply for the 2026 GirlCode Hackathon Across Six African Countries
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
Africa’s technology ecosystem is experiencing unprecedented growth, creating exciting opportunities for innovators to solve real-world challenges through digital solutions. However, despite this progress, women remain significantly underrepresented in the technology industry. To help bridge this gap, GirlCode has launched the 2026 GirlCode Hackathon, one of Africa’s largest women-in-tech hackathons dedicated to empowering female developers, innovators, […]

The post Building Africa’s Next Generation of AI-Powered Female Innovators: Apply for the 2026 GirlCode Hackathon Across Six African Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Africa’s technology ecosystem is experiencing unprecedented growth, creating exciting opportunities for innovators to solve real-world challenges through digital solutions. However, despite this progress, women remain significantly underrepresented in the technology industry. To help bridge this gap, GirlCode has launched the 2026 GirlCode Hackathon, one of Africa’s largest women-in-tech hackathons dedicated to empowering female developers, innovators, entrepreneurs, and aspiring founders.

The annual GirlCode Hackathon is much more than a coding competition. It is a 30-hour innovation marathon designed to give women practical experience, industry exposure, mentorship, networking opportunities, and career pathways while encouraging the development of impactful technology solutions. By bringing together talented women from across six African countries, the hackathon creates an environment where innovation, collaboration, and entrepreneurship thrive.

Whether you are an aspiring software developer, an AI enthusiast, a university student, a recent graduate, or a female entrepreneur with a groundbreaking idea, the GirlCode Hackathon provides an excellent platform to showcase your skills, build innovative products, and connect with leading technology companies and industry experts.

What is the GirlCode Hackathon?

The GirlCode Hackathon is an annual all-women, 30-hour coding marathon that aims to address the gender gap in Africa’s technology sector. It provides participants with an opportunity to work in teams to design, develop, and present innovative technology solutions that solve real-world problems.

Unlike traditional hackathons that focus solely on competition, the GirlCode Hackathon has been structured to create long-term impact by combining technical development with entrepreneurship, mentorship, employment opportunities, and startup acceleration. Participants not only compete for exciting prizes but also gain practical industry experience that can significantly boost their careers.

The hackathon serves as a bridge between education and employment by allowing participants to apply their technical knowledge in a real-world setting while collaborating with like-minded innovators from across the continent.

For more information, visit GIRL CODE website HERE

Empowering Female Tech Founders

For women with innovative business ideas, the GirlCode Hackathon serves as an acceleration platform where concepts can be transformed into market-ready Minimum Viable Products (MVPs).

Developing a working prototype during the hackathon gives founders tangible proof of concept, making it easier to attract investors, secure partnerships, and demonstrate the technical feasibility of their ideas. Participants also receive valuable mentorship and guidance that help strengthen their business models and prepare them for future growth.

This unique approach transforms innovative ideas into scalable technology solutions capable of addressing Africa’s most pressing challenges.

Launching Careers for Female Developers

The GirlCode Hackathon is equally beneficial for aspiring and experienced female developers. Participants gain hands-on experience by working on real projects within multidisciplinary teams, allowing them to strengthen both their technical and professional skills.

Developers benefit from practical coding experience, exposure to startup environments, collaboration with talented peers, mentorship from experienced professionals, networking with industry leaders, and opportunities that may lead to employment.

Rather than simply learning theory, participants leave the event with real project experience that strengthens their portfolios and enhances their competitiveness in the technology job market.

Hackathon Dates and Locations

The 2026 GirlCode Hackathon will take place across six African countries, providing opportunities for women from different regions to participate.

South Africa

  • Johannesburg – MTN Offices (216 14th Avenue)
  • Dates: 15–16 August 2026
  • Cape Town – Venue to be confirmed
  • Dates: 15–16 August 2026
  • APPLY HERE

Botswana

  • Gaborone – Absa Offices
  • Dates: 15–16 August 2026

APPLY HERE

Kenya

  • Nairobi – 7th Floor, Konza Complex, Nairobi-Mombasa Road, Konza Technopolis
  • Dates: 5–6 September 2026

APPLY HERE

Uganda

  • Kampala – UICT Nakawa Campus, New Portbell Road
  • Dates: 5–6 September 2026

APPLY HERE 

Tanzania

  • Dar es Salaam – Absa Offices
  • Dates: 5–6 September 2026

APPLY HERE

Ghana

  • Accra – Absa Offices
  • Dates: 11–12 September 2026

APPLY HERE

Participants are encouraged to apply for the location that is most convenient for them.

Exciting Prizes for Winning Teams

Winning teams from each participating country will receive impressive technology prizes while earning the opportunity to represent their country at the Pan-African Grand Finale.

First Place

The first-place team in each country will receive:

  • Laptop
  • Headphones
  • Power bank
  • You Are a Badass book

In addition, every first-place team advances to the Pan-African Grand Finale.

Second Place

Second-place winners will receive:

  • Tablet
  • Headphones
  • Power bank
  • Keyboard and mouse
  • You Are a Badass book

Third Place

Third-place winners will receive:

  • Headphones
  • Power bank
  • You Are a Badass book

Pan-African Grand Finale

Winning teams from each participating country will compete in the Pan-African Grand Finale for continental recognition.

The overall winning team will receive a cash prize valued at R100,000, making the GirlCode Hackathon one of Africa’s most rewarding women-focused technology competitions.

Six-Month Accelerator Programme

Beyond the cash prize, the Pan-African winning female tech founder will participate in a six-month accelerator programme designed to transform promising prototypes into scalable businesses.

The accelerator programme focuses on:

  • Refining product prototypes.
  • Strengthening business models.
  • Preparing startups for investment opportunities.
  • Scaling innovative technology solutions.
  • Supporting long-term entrepreneurial success.

This additional support ensures that innovative ideas developed during the hackathon continue growing long after the competition ends.

Sponsors Supporting the Initiative

The 2026 GirlCode Hackathon is supported by leading organizations committed to advancing women in technology.

Headliner Sponsor

  • Absa

Venue Sponsor

  • MTN

Platinum Sponsors

  • Truecaller
  • SAP Fioneer

Gold Sponsors

  • Orange Cyberdefense
  • Vuma

Prize Sponsors

  • Jumo
  • Mint Group
  • Datacentrix
  • Mascom

Spot Prize Sponsors

  • Entelect
  • Boxfusion

Community Partner

  • Mobile Web Ghana

The involvement of these organizations demonstrates a shared commitment to creating opportunities for women across Africa’s growing digital economy.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the GirlCode Hackathon offers numerous benefits beyond winning prizes. Participants will gain practical coding experience, develop innovative solutions, strengthen teamwork and leadership skills, receive mentorship from industry professionals, expand their professional networks, build impressive project portfolios, explore employment opportunities, and connect with Africa’s thriving technology ecosystem.

The event also provides aspiring entrepreneurs with an opportunity to validate business ideas, develop functional prototypes, and position themselves for future investment and business growth.

For more AFRICAN opportunities, visit OFY WebsiteHERE

Application Deadline

No official application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the scheduled hackathon dates for their respective countries, as participation may be limited.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official online application form for their preferred hackathon location. Applicants are encouraged to review the event details carefully and submit all required information before the scheduled event dates.

About GirlCode

GirlCode is a non-profit organization dedicated to empowering girls and young women, particularly those from underprivileged backgrounds, by providing technology education, coding skills, mentorship, and career development opportunities. Through initiatives like the GirlCode Hackathon, the organization continues to build a stronger and more inclusive technology ecosystem across Africa.

Conclusion

The 2026 GirlCode Hackathon presents an exceptional opportunity for women across Africa to showcase their technical talents, build innovative technology solutions, gain valuable industry experience, and accelerate their careers in software development, artificial intelligence, and entrepreneurship. Whether you are an aspiring developer looking to enhance your skills or a founder ready to transform an idea into a market-ready product, this hackathon provides the ideal platform to learn, innovate, collaborate, and compete on a continental stage. Women from Ghana, South Africa, Botswana, Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania are encouraged to seize this opportunity and become part of Africa’s next generation of AI-powered female innovators.

 

The post Building Africa’s Next Generation of AI-Powered Female Innovators: Apply for the 2026 GirlCode Hackathon Across Six African Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow, Evidence Project at The Pew Charitable Trusts (Washington, DC): Apply for a High-Impact Science Policy Job
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Pew Charitable Trusts is inviting applications for the Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow, Evidence Project, an exciting full-time opportunity based in Washington, D.C. This role is ideal for professionals passionate about strengthening the connection between scientific research and real-world decision-making. As part of Pew’s Evidence Project, the successful candidate will help bridge the gap […]

The post Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow, Evidence Project at The Pew Charitable Trusts (Washington, DC): Apply for a High-Impact Science Policy Job appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pew Charitable Trusts is inviting applications for the Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow, Evidence Project, an exciting full-time opportunity based in Washington, D.C. This role is ideal for professionals passionate about strengthening the connection between scientific research and real-world decision-making.

As part of Pew’s Evidence Project, the successful candidate will help bridge the gap between research, public policy, and community impact while participating in a prestigious national Civic Science Fellows Program. The position combines strategic engagement, communications, stakeholder management, research, and policy work, making it an outstanding career opportunity for professionals interested in science policy, higher education, civic engagement, and philanthropy.

The position offers a competitive annual salary of USD $79,900–$89,300, comprehensive employee benefits, hybrid work flexibility, and the opportunity to collaborate with university presidents, research funders, government leaders, and scientific institutions across the United States.

About The Pew Charitable Trusts

For more than 75 years, The Pew Charitable Trusts has worked to improve public policy by using evidence, research, and data-driven solutions to address some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

The organization supports initiatives across:

  • Scientific research
  • Public policy
  • Higher education
  • Public health
  • Environmental conservation
  • Marine science
  • Community development
  • Arts and culture
  • Social services

Within Pew, the Philadelphia and Scientific Advancement Portfolio oversees several grantmaking initiatives and research programs, including the Evidence Project, which focuses on ensuring that research more consistently informs policy, practice, and societal outcomes.

About the Evidence Project

The Evidence Project works alongside:

  • Universities
  • Philanthropic organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Civil society organizations
  • Scientific institutions

Its mission is to improve the way research is generated, communicated, and used in solving public challenges.

The project focuses on three major objectives:

  1. Producing actionable evidence on what makes research trusted and useful.
  2. Accelerating adoption of effective research practices across institutions.
  3. Embedding these practices into organizations to create long-term societal impact.

About the Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow Position

The selected candidate will support Pew’s efforts to strengthen research infrastructure for public impact while participating in the Civic Science Fellows Program, a national leadership initiative that prepares emerging leaders working at the intersection of science and society.

Reporting to the Manager, Evidence Project, the fellow will collaborate with university presidents, research funders, and higher education leaders to advance civic science initiatives.

The role is based in Washington, D.C., with a hybrid work schedule that includes office-based collaboration, remote work flexibility, and four telework flex weeks each year.

Key Responsibilities

Successful candidates will contribute to a wide range of strategic activities, including:

Outreach and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Build partnerships with universities, research organizations, and funding agencies.
  • Support engagement strategies that strengthen research partnerships.
  • Promote research with meaningful public impact.

Communications

Develop high-quality communication materials, including:

  • Blog articles
  • Website content
  • Q&A features
  • Social media content
  • Email campaigns
  • Presentation decks
  • Briefing materials

Events and Convenings

Support the planning and implementation of:

  • Virtual roundtables
  • National meetings
  • In-person workshops
  • Stakeholder convenings

Responsibilities include agenda preparation, participant communications, logistics, and background materials.

Relationship Management

Maintain productive relationships with:

  • University leaders
  • Research institutions
  • Scientific organizations
  • Philanthropic funders
  • Civic science partners

Proposal Development

Assist with:

  • Grant proposals
  • Donor reports
  • Project updates
  • Progress documentation

Civic Science Fellowship

As part of the fellowship, the successful candidate will:

  • Design and complete an original civic science project.
  • Help expand implementation of recommendations developed by Pew’s university leadership council.
  • Participate in a national fellowship cohort.
  • Attend fellowship meetings and leadership convenings.

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.
  • Approximately four years of relevant work experience.
  • Strong analytical and research skills.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to summarize complex information clearly.
  • Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills.
  • Experience working collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Ability to apply evidence-based, nonpartisan approaches to policy and research.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft 365.

Preferred Experience

The ideal candidate will also have experience or demonstrated interest in:

  • Science policy
  • Higher education policy
  • Civic science
  • Research impact
  • Public engagement
  • Research organizations
  • Universities
  • Scientific communities
  • Strategic communications
  • Independent project management

Salary and Benefits

The successful applicant will receive:

  • Annual salary of USD $79,900–$89,300
  • Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance
  • Retirement benefits
  • Life and disability insurance
  • Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)
  • Health Savings Account (HSA)
  • Hybrid work arrangement
  • Four annual telework flex weeks
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Participation in the Civic Science Fellows national network

Work Location

  • Washington, D.C., USA
  • Hybrid work arrangement

Travel Requirements

The role includes occasional domestic and international travel to:

  • Conferences
  • Meetings
  • Stakeholder engagements

Work Authorization

Applicants must already have legal authorization to work in the United States.

Visa sponsorship is not available.

Why You Should Apply

This position offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Join one of America’s leading public policy organizations.
  • Work directly with influential universities and scientific institutions.
  • Shape national conversations around evidence-based policymaking.
  • Build leadership experience through the Civic Science Fellows Program.
  • Expand your professional network across research, philanthropy, and higher education.
  • Contribute to improving how science benefits society.

Application Information

Interested candidates should submit their application through The Pew Charitable Trusts Careers Portal.

Apply Here 

Since the vacancy remains open, early application is strongly encouraged.

For more information on this job opportunity, visit here 

Discover more job opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Senior Associate, Civic Science Fellow, Evidence Project at The Pew Charitable Trusts (Washington, DC): Apply for a High-Impact Science Policy Job appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded One Young World Summit Opportunity for Young African AI Leaders
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence is transforming every aspect of modern society—from healthcare and education to governance, climate action, and economic development. As AI continues to shape the future, the need for responsible, ethical, and sustainable innovation has never been greater. To recognize and support the next generation of AI changemakers, The Brandtech Group, in partnership with One […]

The post Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded One Young World Summit Opportunity for Young African AI Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence is transforming every aspect of modern society—from healthcare and education to governance, climate action, and economic development. As AI continues to shape the future, the need for responsible, ethical, and sustainable innovation has never been greater.

To recognize and support the next generation of AI changemakers, The Brandtech Group, in partnership with One Young World, has launched applications for the Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship 2026.

The scholarship is designed to identify five exceptional young African leaders who are using Artificial Intelligence to solve pressing social, environmental, economic, educational, and health challenges across the continent. Successful applicants will receive the opportunity to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, alongside influential global leaders, innovators, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and activists.

If you are creating innovative AI-powered solutions that positively impact your community or country, this scholarship could provide the global platform you need to scale your work and expand your influence.

About the Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship

The Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship celebrates young African innovators who are demonstrating that Artificial Intelligence can be a force for positive change.

While AI offers tremendous opportunities to improve productivity, strengthen healthcare, modernize education, and address climate change, it also presents challenges such as misinformation, online abuse, greenhouse gas emissions from computing infrastructure, and electronic waste.

This scholarship seeks young leaders who are actively ensuring AI is used responsibly to solve real-world problems rather than create new ones.

The initiative reflects The Brandtech Group’s commitment to promoting ethical AI while empowering Africa’s growing population of technology innovators.

Why This Scholarship Matters

Africa has the world’s fastest-growing youth population, yet the continent remains underrepresented in AI research, development, and deployment.

The scholarship recognizes young Africans who are helping bridge this gap by designing AI solutions that promote sustainable development while ensuring technology benefits society responsibly and inclusively.

Selected scholars will also contribute to discussions at the One Young World Summit 2026, where Artificial Intelligence is one of the major global themes.

Scholarship Benefits

Five outstanding scholars will receive the opportunity to participate in the prestigious One Young World Summit 2026.

In addition to joining one of the world’s largest gatherings of young leaders, recipients will benefit from:

  • Global recognition as emerging AI leaders.
  • Participation in the One Young World Summit in Cape Town.
  • Networking with world leaders, business executives, policymakers, investors, entrepreneurs, and fellow innovators.
  • Direct engagement with The Brandtech Group leadership and staff.
  • Opportunities for international collaboration.
  • Exposure to global AI and innovation ecosystems.
  • Enhanced visibility for their AI initiatives.
  • Membership within the global One Young World community.

Priority Focus Areas

Applicants should be leading AI initiatives that contribute to one or more of the following areas:

1. AI for Sustainability and Climate Action

Projects that use Artificial Intelligence to:

  • Protect natural resources.
  • Improve environmental monitoring.
  • Reduce emissions.
  • Advance climate resilience.
  • Promote sustainable development.

2. AI for Education

Solutions that:

  • Expand access to quality education.
  • Improve learning outcomes.
  • Increase educational productivity.
  • Support teachers and students through AI technologies.

3. AI to Combat Misinformation and Promote Social Equity

Initiatives that use AI to:

  • Detect misinformation.
  • Reduce online hate speech.
  • Prevent digital abuse.
  • Promote equality and inclusion.
  • Strengthen safer digital communities.

4. AI for Healthcare

Projects designed to:

  • Improve healthcare access.
  • Address health inequalities.
  • Enhance diagnostics.
  • Strengthen public health systems.
  • Support mental health and wellbeing.

5. AI for Active Citizen Engagement

Solutions that:

  • Increase civic participation.
  • Improve transparency.
  • Strengthen democratic engagement.
  • Enhance communication between governments and citizens.

Applicants whose projects fall outside these five themes may still be considered if they demonstrate the responsible and positive application of Artificial Intelligence for social impact.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be between 18 and 30 years old during the One Young World Summit 2026.
  • Be a national and resident of an African country.
  • Demonstrate strong leadership potential.
  • Show commitment to responsible AI development.
  • Have a proven record of using AI to solve important community or societal challenges.
  • Understand local and global issues affecting society.
  • Be willing to actively participate in scholarship activities and engage with Brandtech Group representatives during the Summit.

Exceptional candidates older than 30 may also be considered if they demonstrate outstanding impact and leadership.

Application Process

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official One Young World application portal.

Applicants who are shortlisted may be requested to submit additional materials, including:

  • References.
  • A video pitch.
  • Supporting documentation demonstrating the impact of their work.

Applicants are encouraged to clearly explain:

  • The problem their AI solution addresses.
  • The measurable impact achieved.
  • Future plans for scaling their innovation.
  • How their work promotes ethical and responsible AI.

Apply Here 

About The Brandtech Group

The Brandtech Group is the world’s leading digital-only marketing technology company and a global leader in generative AI marketing solutions.

The company helps brands improve marketing performance through innovative technology, automation, and responsible AI while supporting initiatives that encourage ethical technological advancement around the world.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 2 August 2026

Interested applicants should submit their applications before the closing date.

Why You Should Apply

The Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship provides much more than access to an international summit. It offers young African innovators the opportunity to showcase their work on a global stage, build strategic partnerships, learn from internationally recognized leaders, and join a lifelong network of changemakers committed to creating positive social impact.

If your AI solution is transforming communities, improving lives, or advancing sustainable development, this scholarship offers an exceptional platform to accelerate your impact.

Conclusion

Artificial Intelligence has the potential to redefine Africa’s future. Through the Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship 2026, five outstanding young African innovators will gain global recognition for using AI responsibly to solve some of the continent’s most pressing challenges.

Whether your work focuses on climate action, healthcare, education, civic engagement, or combating misinformation, this scholarship is an opportunity to amplify your impact, connect with world leaders, and help shape the future of ethical AI.

For more information on this conference scholarship, visit here 

Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY, click here

The post Brandtech Group AI for Good Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded One Young World Summit Opportunity for Young African AI Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Scholarship in Law at the University of Melbourne, Australia 2027: Fully Funded CEVAW Research Opportunity with AUD $41,555 Annual Stipend
item.dc:creator: Bob Waudo
item.description:
Applications are now open for an outstanding PhD Scholarship in Law at the prestigious University of Melbourne through the Australian Research Council (ARC) Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women (CEVAW). This fully funded doctoral research opportunity is designed for highly qualified scholars who are passionate about advancing legal research, gender justice, […]

The post PhD Scholarship in Law at the University of Melbourne, Australia 2027: Fully Funded CEVAW Research Opportunity with AUD $41,555 Annual Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for an outstanding PhD Scholarship in Law at the prestigious University of Melbourne through the Australian Research Council (ARC) Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women (CEVAW). This fully funded doctoral research opportunity is designed for highly qualified scholars who are passionate about advancing legal research, gender justice, public policy, and interdisciplinary approaches to ending violence against women.

Hosted by the Melbourne Law School, the successful PhD candidate will undertake cutting-edge research under the principal supervision of Professor Heather Douglas, a renowned legal scholar in the field. The scholarship provides generous financial support, enabling researchers to focus entirely on producing impactful, evidence-based research that contributes to meaningful policy and societal change.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals seeking to build an academic or professional career in legal research, human rights, gender studies, criminology, public policy, or related disciplines while contributing to one of the world’s most significant research initiatives addressing violence against women.

About the ARC Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women (CEVAW)

The Australian Research Council Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women (CEVAW) is the world’s first Centre dedicated to addressing the complete spectrum of violence against women across Australia and the Indo-Pacific region.

The Centre brings together leading researchers from multiple disciplines to investigate:

  • The structural causes of violence against women.
  • Social, legal, cultural, and institutional factors that contribute to gender-based violence.
  • Evidence-based interventions that can prevent violence.
  • Policy reforms that improve responses to survivors.
  • Innovative research methods that influence governments, institutions, and communities.

Rather than focusing solely on responding to violence after it occurs, CEVAW seeks to understand its root causes and develop long-term solutions that transform systems and reduce violence across societies.

The Centre’s work combines expertise from law, sociology, psychology, public health, Indigenous studies, economics, data science, and public policy, making it one of the most comprehensive research initiatives in this field.

Scholarship Overview

Host Institution

University of Melbourne

Faculty

Melbourne Law School

Research Centre

Australian Research Council Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women (CEVAW)

Degree

Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)

Location

Parkville, Melbourne, Australia

Research Area

Law, Gender Justice, Violence Against Women, Public Policy, Human Rights, Interdisciplinary Research

Expected Start Date

1 March 2027

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support throughout their doctoral studies.

The scholarship includes:

  • Annual stipend of AUD $41,555
  • Scholarship duration of 3.5 years
  • Annual indexation of the stipend
  • Opportunity to conduct research within a globally recognized Centre of Excellence
  • Supervision by Professor Heather Douglas
  • Access to Melbourne Law School’s world-class academic environment
  • Participation in interdisciplinary research collaborations
  • Opportunities to contribute to research that influences public policy and legal reform
  • Access to leading academics, researchers, and international networks

The scholarship allows recipients to devote their full attention to their doctoral research while receiving financial support during the funded period.

Research Focus

The successful PhD candidate will contribute to groundbreaking research examining violence against women through a legal and interdisciplinary lens.

Research conducted under CEVAW focuses on:

  • Structural drivers of violence against women
  • Legal systems and justice responses
  • Gender equality and human rights
  • Prevention strategies
  • Policy development
  • Indigenous-centred research
  • Data-driven evidence
  • Community-based interventions
  • Institutional reform
  • Social justice and legal innovation

Researchers are encouraged to generate new knowledge that strengthens legal frameworks and improves responses to violence against women both nationally and internationally.

Academic Environment

The Melbourne Law School is internationally recognized for excellence in legal education and research.

As a doctoral researcher, you will benefit from:

  • Expert academic supervision
  • Access to extensive research facilities
  • Collaboration with multidisciplinary researchers
  • Participation in seminars and conferences
  • Opportunities for academic publishing
  • Professional development programs
  • Strong research networks across Australia and internationally

Working within CEVAW also provides exposure to policymakers, legal practitioners, government agencies, and organizations working to eliminate violence against women.

Financial Support

Recipients of this scholarship will receive:

  • Annual Stipend: AUD $41,555
  • Duration: 3.5 years
  • Payment: Pro rata
  • Annual Increase: Indexed annually

The funding is intended to support living expenses while undertaking full-time doctoral research.

Who Should Apply?

This scholarship is particularly suited for applicants interested in:

  • Law
  • Human Rights
  • Gender Studies
  • Public Policy
  • Sociology
  • Criminology
  • Legal Research
  • Social Justice
  • Violence Prevention
  • Interdisciplinary Research

Individuals with strong academic records and a demonstrated interest in research that creates real-world impact are encouraged to apply:

APPLY HERE

Why This Scholarship Stands Out

This PhD scholarship offers much more than financial support. It provides an opportunity to become part of one of the world’s leading research initiatives dedicated to eliminating violence against women.

Researchers will have the chance to:

  • Work alongside internationally respected academics.
  • Produce research that informs policy and legal reform.
  • Contribute to evidence-based solutions for complex societal challenges.
  • Build an international research profile.
  • Develop expertise in interdisciplinary legal research.
  • Make meaningful contributions to improving the lives of women and communities.

Few doctoral opportunities combine world-class supervision, generous funding, interdisciplinary collaboration, and the potential to influence public policy on such a significant global issue.

Visit HERE to learn more the PhD Scholarship in Law at the University of Melbourne, Australia 2027

Application Deadline

Deadline: Not specified.

Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before positions are filled.

Conclusion

The University of Melbourne’s PhD Scholarship in Law through the ARC Centre of Excellence for the Elimination of Violence Against Women represents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers committed to advancing legal scholarship and social justice. With generous financial support, access to internationally recognized academics, and the opportunity to contribute to transformative research that shapes policy and practice, this scholarship offers an ideal platform for building a successful academic and professional career. Candidates with a passion for interdisciplinary research and a commitment to addressing violence against women are strongly encouraged to apply for this prestigious fully funded doctoral opportunity.

For more global Scholarship opportunity, visit OFY HERE

The post PhD Scholarship in Law at the University of Melbourne, Australia 2027: Fully Funded CEVAW Research Opportunity with AUD $41,555 Annual Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Reaching Marginalized Communities MIL Conference 2026 in Phnom Penh, Cambodia: A Platform for Youth and Underserved Communities to Shape the Future of Media and Information Literacy.
item.dc:creator: Unnah Mercy
item.description:
Applications are now open for the “Reaching Marginalized Communities” Media and Information Literacy (MIL) Conference 2026, a unique gathering organized by DW Akademie in Phnom Penh, Cambodia. The conference is designed to create an inclusive space where marginalized and underserved communities can share their experiences, discuss pressing media-related challenges, and contribute to conversations that influence […]

The post Reaching Marginalized Communities MIL Conference 2026 in Phnom Penh, Cambodia: A Platform for Youth and Underserved Communities to Shape the Future of Media and Information Literacy. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the “Reaching Marginalized Communities” Media and Information Literacy (MIL) Conference 2026, a unique gathering organized by DW Akademie in Phnom Penh, Cambodia. The conference is designed to create an inclusive space where marginalized and underserved communities can share their experiences, discuss pressing media-related challenges, and contribute to conversations that influence more equitable access to information.

Scheduled to take place on Sunday, 25 October 2026, this half-day conference will bring together participants from across Cambodia, particularly individuals representing communities that are often excluded from mainstream discussions. With a special focus on young people, the event aims to empower participants through dialogue, knowledge exchange, and collaborative learning on critical media and information literacy issues.

One of the major highlights of the conference is that travel expenses for participants coming from outside Phnom Penh will be fully covered, making it easier for individuals from rural and remote areas to attend.

The event will be conducted in both English and Khmer, with simultaneous interpretation available to ensure that language is not a barrier to meaningful participation.

About the Reaching Marginalized Communities MIL Conference

The Reaching Marginalized Communities Media and Information Literacy Conference is an initiative by DW Akademie that seeks to strengthen inclusive participation in discussions surrounding media, digital communication, and access to reliable information.

As technology rapidly transforms the way societies communicate, marginalized populations often face additional barriers to accessing trustworthy information, expressing themselves freely, and protecting themselves from misinformation and online threats.

This conference aims to bridge those gaps by creating a safe and inclusive environment where participants can:

  • Share experiences from their communities.
  • Discuss challenges affecting marginalized populations.
  • Learn from experts and peers.
  • Exchange ideas for improving media and information literacy.
  • Build networks with organizations and community representatives.
  • Promote inclusive digital participation.

Conference Date and Venue

  • Event Date: Sunday, 25 October 2026
  • Location: Phnom Penh, Cambodia
  • Duration: Half-day conference

Conference Themes

Participants will explore several important topics related to media and information literacy, including:

  • Freedom of expression
  • Access to reliable information
  • Combating misinformation and disinformation
  • Hate speech and online discrimination
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Online scams and digital safety
  • Media literacy education
  • Digital inclusion
  • Community participation in media
  • Responsible use of online platforms

These discussions will help participants understand how media affects their communities while equipping them with practical knowledge to navigate today’s digital environment.

Communities the Conference Seeks to Engage

DW Akademie is particularly encouraging participation from representatives of marginalized and underserved populations, including:

  • Ethnic minority communities
  • Indigenous communities
  • Rural communities
  • Remote communities
  • Fishing communities
  • Religious minorities
  • Refugees
  • Displaced persons
  • Returnees
  • Persons with disabilities
  • LGBTQ+ communities
  • Garment workers
  • Factory workers
  • People living in poverty
  • Individuals living with mental health conditions
  • Other underserved populations

The conference also welcomes organizations and community leaders working closely with these groups.

Special Focus on Youth

Young people remain one of the primary focus groups for this conference.

Youth representatives are encouraged to actively contribute to discussions, share community experiences, and participate in building more inclusive media environments.

Applicants aged 15–35 years are specifically recognized within the application form.

Opportunity to Deliver a Community Presentation

Participants who wish to contribute beyond attending discussions may volunteer to give a 10–15 minute presentation during the conference.

This presentation provides an opportunity to:

  • Introduce your community.
  • Explain the challenges your community faces.
  • Share successful initiatives.
  • Discuss media and information literacy needs.
  • Highlight opportunities for collaboration.
  • Raise awareness about underserved populations.

Applicants can indicate their interest in presenting during the application process.

Conference Languages

To ensure broad participation, the conference will be conducted in:

  • English
  • Khmer

Simultaneous interpretation will be provided throughout the event.

Travel Support

One of the most significant benefits offered by DW Akademie is financial support for participants traveling from outside Phnom Penh.

Eligible participants living in other provinces of Cambodia will have their travel expenses covered, helping reduce financial barriers to participation.

What Applicants Will Be Asked During Registration

The application form requests information including:

  • Full name
  • Date of birth
  • Gender
  • Phone number
  • Email address
  • Province or current location in Cambodia
  • Community or organization represented
  • Position within the organization or community
  • Whether the applicant belongs to specific demographic groups
  • Whether the applicant wishes to deliver a short presentation
  • Description of the represented community or NGO

Applicants should complete the form as accurately as possible.

DW Akademie states that all submitted information will be treated confidentially.

Benefits of Participating

Selected participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Participate in a national media and information literacy conference.
  • Engage with representatives from diverse marginalized communities.
  • Exchange ideas with civil society organizations and community leaders.
  • Learn practical approaches to addressing misinformation and digital challenges.
  • Strengthen understanding of AI and digital media.
  • Build valuable professional and community networks.
  • Increase awareness of freedom of expression and access to information.
  • Present their community’s work and challenges (optional).
  • Receive travel support if traveling from outside Phnom Penh.
  • Participate in bilingual discussions through English-Khmer interpretation.

Why This Conference Matters

Media and information literacy has become increasingly important as digital platforms continue to shape public discourse worldwide.

Marginalized communities frequently face obstacles including limited access to credible information, online discrimination, misinformation, hate speech, and digital exclusion.

This conference provides a platform where these communities can voice their concerns, share solutions, and collectively strengthen their capacity to engage safely and effectively in today’s information landscape.

By centering the experiences of underserved populations, DW Akademie aims to encourage more inclusive media systems that leave no community behind.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application form by providing all requested information.

Applicants who would like to give a presentation should indicate their interest in the application form and prepare a presentation lasting between 10 and 15 minutes.

All personal information submitted through the application will be handled confidentially by the organizers.

APPLY HERE.

Application Deadline

Deadline: Not specified

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible.

CLICK HERE for more information.

CONCLUSION.

The Reaching Marginalized Communities Media and Information Literacy Conference 2026 represents an important opportunity for individuals, youth leaders, civil society organizations, and representatives of marginalized communities across Cambodia to engage in meaningful conversations about media, digital inclusion, and equitable access to information.

By bringing together participants from diverse backgrounds, the conference seeks to strengthen collaboration, amplify underrepresented voices, and promote more inclusive approaches to media and information literacy. With travel support available for participants outside Phnom Penh, bilingual interpretation, and opportunities to present community experiences, the event offers an accessible platform for learning, networking, and advocacy.

Individuals who are passionate about empowering marginalized communities and advancing media literacy are encouraged to apply and become part of this impactful initiative.

Visit HERE for more OFY conference opportunities.

The post Reaching Marginalized Communities MIL Conference 2026 in Phnom Penh, Cambodia: A Platform for Youth and Underserved Communities to Shape the Future of Media and Information Literacy. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026: USDA Offers Up to $1 Million to Strengthen Rural Cooperatives Across the United States
item.dc:creator: Richard Nyirenda
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), through the National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA), has opened applications for the Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026. The program provides substantial grant funding ranging from $200,000 to $1 million to eligible nonprofit organizations that establish and operate Centers for Rural Cooperative Development, helping strengthen rural […]

The post Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026: USDA Offers Up to $1 Million to Strengthen Rural Cooperatives Across the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), through the National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA), has opened applications for the Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026. The program provides substantial grant funding ranging from $200,000 to $1 million to eligible nonprofit organizations that establish and operate Centers for Rural Cooperative Development, helping strengthen rural economies through cooperative business development.

With an estimated $5 million in total program funding, the RCDG Program supports organizations that provide technical assistance to rural cooperatives and mutually owned businesses. By improving access to business expertise, organizational development, and cooperative education, the initiative seeks to create stronger rural enterprises, generate employment opportunities, and improve economic conditions across rural America.

Applications are now open, with interested organizations required to submit their applications by July 24, 2026.

About the Rural Cooperative Development Grants Program

The Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program is designed to support nonprofit institutions that establish and operate Centers for Rural Cooperative Development. These centers play an important role in helping rural communities create, expand, and strengthen cooperative businesses.

Rather than providing direct financial assistance to individual cooperatives, the program funds nonprofit organizations that deliver expert technical assistance, business counseling, training, and educational support to rural cooperatives and mutually owned enterprises.

The ultimate objective is to improve economic opportunities in rural areas by encouraging sustainable business development through the cooperative business model.

Program Objectives

The Rural Cooperative Development Grants Program aims to:

  • Strengthen rural economies through cooperative business development.
  • Support the creation of new cooperatives in rural communities.
  • Assist existing cooperatives in expanding their operations.
  • Improve the long-term sustainability of mutually owned businesses.
  • Increase employment and economic opportunities in rural America.
  • Provide expert technical assistance to rural entrepreneurs and organizations.
  • Enhance the capacity of nonprofit organizations serving rural communities.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive grant funding ranging between:

  • Minimum Award: $200,000
  • Maximum Award: $1,000,000

The USDA has allocated an estimated $5,000,000 in total funding for this funding cycle.

Applicants should note that the program includes a matching fund requirement. Details regarding matching contributions are provided in the official Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO).

Who Can Apply?

Applications will only be accepted from eligible nonprofit institutions.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Nonprofit organizations.
  • Accredited nonprofit institutions of higher education.
  • Other nonprofit institutions that satisfy all eligibility requirements established under federal regulations.

A nonprofit institution is defined as an organization or institution whose net earnings do not benefit any private shareholder or individual.

Applicants must also satisfy all eligibility requirements established under 7 CFR 4284.520(a).

Organizations that meet any of the ineligibility conditions described in 7 CFR 4284.520(b) will not be considered for funding.

APPLY HERE

What Can the Grant Support?

Grant funding supports nonprofit organizations that establish and operate Centers for Rural Cooperative Development.

These centers provide technical assistance that may include:

  • Assisting communities in establishing new cooperatives.
  • Supporting the expansion of existing cooperatives.
  • Improving the operations of rural cooperative businesses.
  • Delivering business planning and management support.
  • Providing cooperative education and training.
  • Strengthening governance and organizational capacity.
  • Promoting sustainable rural economic development.

Through these services, nonprofit organizations help rural communities build stronger businesses and create lasting economic opportunities.

Why the Program Matters

Cooperatives have long played a significant role in supporting rural economies by allowing individuals, producers, and businesses to work together toward shared economic goals.

However, many rural communities lack access to the specialized technical expertise needed to establish and successfully manage cooperative enterprises.

The Rural Cooperative Development Grants Program addresses this challenge by investing in organizations capable of delivering professional guidance and technical assistance to rural communities.

By strengthening local cooperative development centers, the program contributes to:

  • Increased rural entrepreneurship.
  • Improved business sustainability.
  • Stronger local economies.
  • Job creation.
  • Expanded economic opportunities.
  • Community resilience.

Matching Requirement

The Rural Cooperative Development Grants Program requires applicants to provide matching contributions toward project costs.

Applicants should carefully review the official NOFO for detailed information regarding eligible matching contributions and compliance requirements.

Failure to meet the matching requirement may affect funding eligibility.

Application Process

Eligible nonprofit organizations must submit their applications in accordance with the instructions outlined in the official Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO).

Before applying, applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Carefully review the complete NOFO.
  2. Confirm eligibility requirements.
  3. Prepare all required documentation.
  4. Ensure compliance with federal grant regulations.
  5. Submit applications before the closing date.

Organizations that are new to federal funding opportunities are encouraged to review USDA NIFA’s grant guidance to better understand the federal award process.

Application Review

Applications will be evaluated based on the eligibility requirements and evaluation criteria described in the official NOFO.

Applicants that fail to meet program eligibility requirements by the application deadline may be excluded from consideration.

Only complete applications submitted by eligible nonprofit institutions will be reviewed for funding.

Visit HERE to learn more about Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026

Reasonable Accommodation and Language Support

The USDA is committed to ensuring equal access to funding opportunities.

Applicants requiring reasonable accommodations should contact the information contact listed in the funding announcement at least 10 days before the application deadline.

In addition, free language access services including interpretation and translation of essential information are available upon request. Requests for language services should also be submitted no later than 10 days before the closing date.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: July 7, 2026
  • Application Deadline: July 24, 2026
  • Funding Opportunity Number: USDA-NIFA-OP-012058
  • Assistance Listing Number: 10.771

Grant Summary

  • Funding Organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA)
  • Program: Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG)
  • Funding Available: $5,000,000
  • Award Range: $200,000–$1,000,000
  • Matching Requirement: Yes
  • Eligible Applicants: Nonprofit institutions
  • Application Deadline: July 24, 2026

Conclusion

The Rural Cooperative Development Grants Program 2026 presents an important funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations committed to strengthening rural economies through cooperative development. With awards ranging from $200,000 to $1 million, the program enables eligible organizations to establish and operate Centers for Rural Cooperative Development that provide critical technical assistance to rural cooperatives and mutually owned businesses.

Organizations interested in applying should carefully review the program requirements, confirm their eligibility, prepare a competitive application, and submit all required materials before the July 24, 2026 deadline. Given the significant impact these grants can have on rural communities, eligible nonprofit institutions are encouraged to begin the application process as early as possible.

For more global grant opportunities visit OFY HERE

The post Rural Cooperative Development Grants (RCDG) Program 2026: USDA Offers Up to $1 Million to Strengthen Rural Cooperatives Across the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NYU Grossman School of Medicine Research Coordinator Job 2026 in the USA | Full-Time Population Health Research Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The NYU Grossman School of Medicine, one of the leading medical schools in the United States, is inviting applications for a full-time Research Coordinator position at the Center for Opioid Epidemiology and Policy (COEP) within the Department of Population Health. This exciting career opportunity offers talented professionals the chance to contribute to cutting-edge public health […]

The post NYU Grossman School of Medicine Research Coordinator Job 2026 in the USA | Full-Time Population Health Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NYU Grossman School of Medicine, one of the leading medical schools in the United States, is inviting applications for a full-time Research Coordinator position at the Center for Opioid Epidemiology and Policy (COEP) within the Department of Population Health. This exciting career opportunity offers talented professionals the chance to contribute to cutting-edge public health research that addresses substance use, mental health, stigma, health equity, and overdose prevention.

Working under the supervision of Dr. Suzan Walters, Assistant Professor at the Center for Opioid Epidemiology and Policy and Director of the Resilience, Intersectional Stigma, and Empowerment (RISE) Lab, the successful candidate will support multiple National Institutes of Health (NIH)-funded research projects and other impactful studies focused on improving health outcomes for vulnerable populations.

This position is ideal for individuals passionate about public health, epidemiology, medical research, health policy, sociology, psychology, or related disciplines who wish to build a meaningful research career while working at one of America’s premier academic medical institutions.

About NYU Grossman School of Medicine

For over 175 years, the NYU Grossman School of Medicine has remained at the forefront of medical education, scientific discovery, and patient care. As part of NYU Langone Health, the institution has educated thousands of physicians, researchers, and healthcare leaders who have made lasting contributions to medicine and public health worldwide.

NYU Grossman School of Medicine is recognized for:

  • World-class medical education
  • Innovative biomedical and public health research
  • Commitment to patient-centered care
  • Diverse and inclusive workplace
  • Strong emphasis on equity and belonging
  • Collaborative academic environment

The institution values diversity and strives to ensure that faculty, staff, researchers, and students from all backgrounds can thrive while contributing their unique skills, knowledge, and perspectives.

About the Research Coordinator Position

The Research Coordinator will join the Center for Opioid Epidemiology and Policy (COEP) and work directly with Dr. Suzan Walters on several NIH-funded and other externally funded research projects.

The position combines scientific research, project coordination, administrative management, and stakeholder engagement.

The successful candidate will contribute to studies examining:

  • Social determinants of health
  • Structural determinants affecting healthcare access
  • Substance use
  • Opioid epidemiology
  • Mental health
  • Harm reduction
  • Stigma
  • Health policy
  • Community-based research

This role offers an outstanding opportunity to gain experience in multidisciplinary public health research while collaborating with leading researchers, analysts, doctoral students, postdoctoral fellows, and faculty members.

About the RISE Lab

The successful applicant will support the work of the Resilience, Intersectional Stigma, and Empowerment (RISE) Lab.

The laboratory focuses on understanding how different forms of stigma affect:

  • Access to healthcare
  • Engagement with treatment
  • Mental health
  • Substance use recovery
  • Overall health outcomes

Its research aims to improve services and develop evidence-based interventions for individuals facing substance use disorders and other health challenges.

About the Center for Opioid Epidemiology and Policy (COEP)

COEP serves as a leading research center focused on understanding and responding to the rapidly evolving opioid overdose epidemic in the United States.

Its mission includes:

  • Conducting high-quality research
  • Informing evidence-based policies
  • Preventing opioid misuse
  • Reducing overdose deaths
  • Improving treatment access
  • Supporting national and global public health efforts

As part of COEP, the Research Coordinator will participate in:

  • Monthly analyst meetings
  • Journal clubs
  • Research seminars
  • Career development sessions
  • Faculty collaborations
  • Scientific discussions
  • Professional networking opportunities

Key Responsibilities

The Research Coordinator will play an important role across several stages of the research process.

Responsibilities include:

Research Activities

  • Participate in participant recruitment and outreach.
  • Conduct informed consent procedures.
  • Administer surveys and interviews.
  • Support primary data collection.
  • Conduct literature reviews.
  • Assist with qualitative and quantitative data analysis.
  • Prepare scientific manuscripts.
  • Draft policy briefs.
  • Prepare grant proposals.

Project Management

The successful candidate will:

  • Track project timelines.
  • Monitor deliverables.
  • Coordinate meetings.
  • Prepare meeting agendas.
  • Record meeting notes.
  • Follow up on action items.
  • Maintain organized project documentation.

Regulatory Compliance

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing Institutional Review Board (IRB) submissions.
  • Preparing grant documentation.
  • Maintaining research compliance.
  • Reporting study progress.

Data Management

Applicants will also:

  • Manage REDCap databases.
  • Administer research surveys.
  • Track participants.
  • Conduct data quality checks.
  • Generate reports.
  • Maintain research databases.

Administrative Support

The coordinator will:

  • Coordinate service agreements.
  • Order research supplies.
  • Process study payments.
  • Maintain compliance with institutional financial policies.

Scientific Communication

The successful applicant will help:

  • Prepare presentations.
  • Create research summaries.
  • Develop data visualizations.
  • Produce reports for collaborators.
  • Translate scientific findings for non-academic audiences.

Event Coordination

Responsibilities also include:

  • Organizing scientific meetings.
  • Coordinating workshops.
  • Supporting collaborative research events.

Minimum Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, Allied Health, or a related discipline.
  • At least 2 years of relevant experience, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • Excellent written communication skills.
  • Strong verbal communication abilities.
  • Effective interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work independently under supervision.
  • Strong problem-solving abilities.
  • Excellent organizational skills.
  • Time management skills.
  • Ability to work under pressure.

Applicants should also have proficiency with:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft Access
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Microsoft Outlook
  • Internet-based research tools

Preferred Qualifications

NYU particularly encourages applicants with:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Public Health, Sociology, Psychology, Policy, or related fields.
  • Two or more years of public health or academic research experience.
  • Experience recruiting research participants.
  • Experience with community-based research.
  • Knowledge of quantitative or qualitative data analysis software such as:
    • Stata
    • R
    • SAS
    • NVivo
  • Experience related to:
    • Substance use
    • Harm reduction
    • Overdose prevention
    • Public health systems
    • Health policy
  • Excellent scientific writing skills.
  • Ability to communicate research findings to non-academic audiences.
  • Strong organizational abilities.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Salary

NYU Langone Health has published the annual salary range for this role.

Annual Salary:

  • USD 66,300 – USD 72,345

Actual compensation will depend on factors such as:

  • Experience
  • Education
  • Specialty
  • Institutional needs

The salary does not include bonuses, incentives, or additional compensation.

Employee Benefits

NYU Grossman School of Medicine offers a comprehensive employee benefits package designed to support staff throughout every stage of life.

Benefits include:

  • Comprehensive wellness programs
  • Financial security benefits
  • Retirement support
  • Generous paid time off
  • Employee resource groups
  • Career development opportunities
  • Family support resources
  • Physical wellness programs
  • Mental health support
  • Nutritional wellness initiatives
  • Sleep wellness resources
  • Social well-being programs
  • Preventive healthcare support

Employees also benefit from a collaborative academic environment that encourages professional growth and lifelong learning.

Diversity and Equal Opportunity

NYU Grossman School of Medicine is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants receive equal consideration for employment regardless of background or identity.

The institution values diversity and encourages applicants from all communities to apply.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete an online application through the official NYU Langone Health careers portal.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application highlighting their research experience, project management skills, analytical abilities, and commitment to improving public health through evidence-based research.

Apply Here

Deadline

No application deadline has been specified. Applications remain open until the position is filled or removed from the recruitment portal. So applicants are encouraged to apply early.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post NYU Grossman School of Medicine Research Coordinator Job 2026 in the USA | Full-Time Population Health Research Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Future Voices Program 2026/27: Apply for Climate Change Journalism and Media Leadership Training in Southeast Asia
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Future Voices Program 2026/27, a leadership and professional development initiative for early- to mid-career media professionals across Southeast Asia. This year’s program focuses on climate change journalism, providing participants with opportunities to strengthen their reporting skills, develop their leadership capacity, and connect with a regional network of emerging media […]

The post Future Voices Program 2026/27: Apply for Climate Change Journalism and Media Leadership Training in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Future Voices Program 2026/27, a leadership and professional development initiative for early- to mid-career media professionals across Southeast Asia. This year’s program focuses on climate change journalism, providing participants with opportunities to strengthen their reporting skills, develop their leadership capacity, and connect with a regional network of emerging media professionals.

The Future Voices Program is delivered by ABC International Development, also known as ABCID, with support from the ASEAN-Australia Centre. It is designed for media professionals who want to improve the quality, impact, and ethical foundations of climate reporting within their newsrooms and communities.

A total of 33 media professionals will be selected for the 2026/27 cohort. Participants will take part in online learning, professional development activities over the course of a year, and continued engagement through the program’s alumni network.

From the full cohort, 11 participants will later be selected to attend an intensive in-country program in Australia from 20 to 30 October 2026.

Applications must be submitted by 25 July 2026.

About the Future Voices Program

The Future Voices Program is a regional media leadership initiative created to support emerging professionals working in journalism and the wider media sector across Southeast Asia.

The program recognises that climate change is not only an environmental issue. It also affects economies, public health, migration, food security, energy systems, livelihoods, governance, and community resilience. Media professionals therefore play an important role in helping the public understand climate-related developments and the consequences they may have for different communities.

Through the 2026/27 program, participants will be encouraged to strengthen their ability to report on climate change accurately, responsibly, and ethically. They will also develop leadership skills that can help them influence newsroom practices, guide colleagues, engage audiences, and contribute to stronger public-interest journalism.

The program combines several forms of professional development, including:

  • Online learning activities

  • Leadership development

  • Climate change journalism training

  • Professional networking

  • Ongoing alumni engagement

  • Opportunities for selected participants to undertake advanced training in Australia

Rather than focusing only on short-term training, Future Voices offers a longer professional development journey. Participants will remain involved in the program for approximately one year, allowing them to apply what they learn within their own professional environments.

Future Voices Program 2026/27 Theme: Climate Change Journalism

The central theme of the 2026/27 intake is climate change journalism.

Climate reporting often requires journalists to explain complex scientific, economic, political, and social issues in language that is accessible to the public. It also requires careful verification, ethical judgment, and an understanding of how climate-related developments affect different groups of people.
