The Future Voices Program aims to strengthen climate reporting across both newsrooms and communities in Southeast Asia. Participants will have the opportunity to improve how they identify, investigate, and communicate climate stories.

The program may be particularly relevant to media professionals whose work involves topics such as:

  • Climate change and environmental policy

  • Renewable energy and energy transitions

  • Extreme weather and natural disasters

  • Community resilience

  • Sustainable development

  • Agriculture and food security

  • Climate-related displacement

  • Public health and environmental risks

  • Government and corporate climate commitments

  • The social and economic effects of climate change

Applicants do not necessarily need to work exclusively as climate journalists. The program is intended for emerging media professionals who are ready to strengthen their understanding of climate reporting and use that knowledge within their wider professional work.

How Many Participants Will Be Selected?

ABC International Development will select a cohort of 33 media professionals from across Southeast Asia.

All 33 participants will join the broader Future Voices professional development program, which includes online learning, year-long development activities, and ongoing connections with the program’s alumni community.

Of these 33 participants, 11 will be selected to attend an intensive in-country course in Australia.

Being selected for the initial cohort does not automatically guarantee participation in the Australia-based component. The Australian intensive course is a further opportunity available to a smaller group chosen from among the 33 participants.

Future Voices Program Structure

The program has several interconnected components designed to support participants at different stages of their professional development.

1. Online Learning

Participants will take part in online learning activities focused on climate journalism, leadership, and professional development.

The online format will allow media professionals from different parts of Southeast Asia to participate while continuing their regular work. It will also create opportunities for participants to exchange experiences across different media systems, communities, and national contexts.

2. One Year of Professional Development

The Future Voices Program is not limited to a single workshop or training session. Selected participants will receive approximately one year of professional development support.

This extended structure gives participants time to reflect on the training, test new approaches, and apply their learning in their workplaces and communities.

Participants may also have opportunities to build relationships with other media professionals facing similar reporting challenges across the region.

3. Regional Networking

The program brings together emerging media leaders from Southeast Asia, creating a professional network that extends beyond individual newsrooms or countries.

Participants will be able to connect with journalists and media professionals who bring different experiences, perspectives, and areas of expertise.

This regional network may support future collaboration, knowledge-sharing, cross-border reporting, and continued professional exchange.

4. Alumni Engagement

Ongoing alumni engagement is another important part of the Future Voices Program.

Participants will not simply complete the program and lose contact with the network. Instead, they will become part of a wider alumni community that can provide continued opportunities for professional connection and regional engagement.

This long-term alumni element reflects the program’s aim of developing a sustained community of emerging media leaders.

Australia Intensive Course for 11 Selected Participants

Eleven participants from the cohort will be selected to travel to Australia for an intensive in-country course from 20 to 30 October 2026.

The Australia component will provide additional academic, professional, and networking opportunities.

During the intensive course, selected participants will:

Earn a Leadership and Climate Change Micro-Credential

Participants will complete a leadership and climate change micro-credential at a leading Australian university.

A micro-credential is a focused academic or professional qualification covering a specialised area of knowledge. This component will allow participants to deepen their understanding of climate change while also developing practical leadership capabilities.

Receive Training at the Australian Broadcasting Corporation

Participants will receive professional training at the Australian Broadcasting Corporation, commonly known as the ABC.

The training will expose participants to the practices and professional experience of Australia’s national public broadcaster. It may also help participants reflect on newsroom leadership, ethical journalism, public-interest reporting, and methods of communicating complex climate issues to broad audiences.

Attend the All-Energy Australia Conference

The selected participants will attend the All-Energy Australia conference.

The conference brings together people working across the energy and climate sectors. Attendance will provide participants with an opportunity to learn about developments in renewable energy, energy policy, technology, and the broader transition toward lower-carbon systems.

For journalists, exposure to climate and energy experts can support more informed reporting and help them identify potential sources, story ideas, and emerging regional issues.

Connect With Australian Climate Institutions

Participants will have opportunities to engage with major Australian climate institutions.

These connections may help participants gain a broader understanding of climate research, policy, advocacy, and communication. They may also allow participants to compare Australian approaches with the climate challenges and policy debates taking place in Southeast Asia.

Develop a Personalised Leadership Action Project

Each selected participant will develop a personalised leadership action project with support from industry mentors.

The action project will allow participants to translate the program’s training into a practical initiative connected to their own professional goals.

Depending on the participant’s role and newsroom context, such a project could involve strengthening climate coverage, introducing new editorial practices, developing a reporting initiative, improving audience engagement, or supporting professional learning within a media organisation.

The exact format of each project will be shaped by the participant’s professional responsibilities and development objectives.

Who Should Apply?

The Future Voices Program is intended for early- to mid-career media professionals in Southeast Asia.

It is particularly suitable for applicants who are interested in developing their leadership abilities and improving the quality of climate journalism within their organisations or communities.

Potential applicants may include professionals working in areas such as:

  • Journalism

  • News reporting

  • Editing

  • Broadcasting

  • Digital media

  • Radio

  • Television

  • Multimedia production

  • Newsroom management

  • Media development

  • Public-interest communication

The original announcement does not provide a complete list of accepted job titles. Applicants should therefore consider whether their current work clearly places them within the media profession and whether they can demonstrate how the program relates to their professional development.

English-Language Requirement

Applicants must be proficient in English.

Because the program includes regional interaction, online learning, professional training, and possible participation in the Australia-based intensive course, participants will need sufficient English-language ability to communicate effectively and take part in all program activities.

The announcement does not specify whether applicants must submit an English-language certificate or test result. Applicants should carefully review the official application form for any additional instructions concerning evidence of English proficiency.

What Makes a Strong Applicant?

Although the announcement does not provide detailed selection criteria, the program is likely to appeal most strongly to applicants who can demonstrate a clear connection between their professional experience and the aims of Future Voices.

A strong application should clearly explain:

  1. The applicant’s current role in journalism or the media sector.

  2. Their experience as an early- or mid-career media professional.

  3. Their interest in climate change journalism.

  4. The leadership skills they hope to develop.

  5. How the program could improve their work or strengthen their newsroom.

  6. How they would use the knowledge gained to benefit their community or audience.

  7. Their willingness to participate in online learning and longer-term professional development.

  8. Their ability to communicate and participate in English.

Applicants should avoid relying on general statements about being passionate or motivated. They should instead use concrete examples from their professional experience.

For example, applicants could describe a climate-related story they have reported, an editorial challenge they have faced, a gap in climate coverage within their newsroom, or a leadership responsibility they would like to take on.

Why Apply for the Future Voices Program?

The Future Voices Program offers more than technical journalism training. It combines climate reporting, leadership development, regional networking, and long-term professional engagement.

Successful applicants may benefit from:

  • Greater knowledge of climate change and climate communication

  • Improved reporting and editorial skills

  • Stronger ethical journalism practices

  • Leadership development

  • Connections with media professionals across Southeast Asia

  • Access to a continuing alumni network

  • Opportunities to learn from Australian institutions

  • Potential selection for the intensive course in Australia

  • Academic recognition through a micro-credential for participants selected for the Australia component

  • Mentorship while developing a leadership action project

The program may be especially valuable for professionals seeking to take on greater responsibility within their newsrooms or to become stronger voices in climate journalism.

Important Program Dates

Applicants should note the following dates:

  • Applications opened: 30 June 2026
  • Announcement updated: 1 July 2026
  • Application deadline: 25 July 2026
  • Australia intensive course: 20–30 October 2026
  • Program intake: 2026/27

Applicants should submit their applications before the deadline rather than waiting until the final day. Early preparation provides time to review written responses, confirm eligibility, and address any technical problems with the application platform.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must apply through the official Future Voices Program application link provided by ABC International Development.

Before beginning the application, candidates should prepare information about their:

  • Current professional role
  • Media and journalism experience
  • Leadership experience or potential
  • Interest in climate change reporting
  • Professional development goals
  • Expected contribution to the regional network
  • English-language ability
  • Availability to participate in program activities

Applicants should carefully review every section of the official application form because additional questions, document requirements, or selection conditions may appear there.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Future Voices Program 2026/27 is 25 July 2026.

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. Candidates are advised to confirm the deadline according to the time zone stated on the official application portal, where applicable.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Conclusion

The Future Voices Program 2026/27 offers an important professional development opportunity for emerging media leaders across Southeast Asia who want to strengthen climate change journalism.

With 33 participants joining a year-long program and 11 receiving the opportunity to attend an intensive course in Australia, Future Voices combines regional learning with advanced leadership training, institutional engagement, and professional networking.

The program is particularly relevant at a time when communities need climate journalism that is accurate, ethical, accessible, and grounded in the realities of people affected by environmental change.

Early- and mid-career media professionals who are proficient in English and committed to improving climate reporting should prepare and submit their applications before 25 July 2026.

Discover more global job opportunities, click here.

The post Future Voices Program 2026/27: Apply for Climate Change Journalism and Media Leadership Training in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: A Million Realities Fellowship 2027: Nominate Yourself or an Inspiring Youth Leader for a Two-Year Leadership Programme in the UK
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
The A Million Realities (AMR) Fellowship 2027 is now accepting nominations for its next cohort of outstanding leaders and emerging leaders who are committed to improving the lives of young people in the United Kingdom. This prestigious two-year leadership programme brings together individuals from across the youth sector to collaborate, strengthen their leadership capacity, and […]

The post A Million Realities Fellowship 2027: Nominate Yourself or an Inspiring Youth Leader for a Two-Year Leadership Programme in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The A Million Realities (AMR) Fellowship 2027 is now accepting nominations for its next cohort of outstanding leaders and emerging leaders who are committed to improving the lives of young people in the United Kingdom. This prestigious two-year leadership programme brings together individuals from across the youth sector to collaborate, strengthen their leadership capacity, and drive meaningful change for young people transitioning into adulthood.

Unlike many fellowship programmes that rely solely on direct applications, the AMR Fellowship begins with a nomination process. Whether you are nominated by someone who recognizes your leadership or choose to nominate yourself, this programme offers an exceptional opportunity to join a network of influential changemakers dedicated to creating a better future for young people.

Nominations are open from July to September 2026, with the successful Fellows beginning the programme in March 2027.

About the A Million Realities Fellowship

The A Million Realities Fellowship is designed to identify and support leaders who are making a significant contribution to improving outcomes for young people across the UK.

The Fellowship brings together individuals working in a wide range of sectors connected to youth development and wellbeing. Participants come from:

  • Frontline youth organisations
  • Education
  • Public policy
  • Mental health services
  • Housing
  • Philanthropy
  • Community organisations
  • Social enterprises
  • Charities
  • Youth advocacy initiatives

Rather than focusing on the size or profile of an organisation, the Fellowship emphasizes the quality of leadership, demonstrated impact, commitment to young people, and the potential to influence positive change.

The programme creates an environment where Fellows can strengthen their leadership abilities, exchange ideas, collaborate across sectors, and contribute to long-term improvements in youth development.

Why the A Million Realities Fellowship Matters

Young people face increasingly complex challenges as they transition into adulthood. These challenges may include access to education, employment, housing, mental health support, social inclusion, and economic opportunity.

The A Million Realities Fellowship seeks leaders who are already working to address these issues and provides them with a platform to amplify their impact.

By investing in leaders rather than projects alone, the Fellowship aims to strengthen the wider youth ecosystem and encourage collaboration between professionals from diverse backgrounds.

Participants become part of a growing community of leaders committed to shaping policies, services, and initiatives that improve the lives of young people throughout the UK.

Who Are the A Million Realities Fellows?

A Million Realities Fellows represent a broad spectrum of professionals and emerging leaders whose work contributes to youth development.

The Fellowship welcomes individuals working in areas such as:

  • Youth development
  • Education
  • Community leadership
  • Public policy
  • Youth mental health
  • Housing and homelessness
  • Social justice
  • Philanthropy
  • Non-profit leadership
  • Community engagement
  • Youth empowerment
  • Social innovation

What unites Fellows is their commitment to creating opportunities for young people and their demonstrated ability to lead with purpose and impact.

The Nomination Process

One distinctive feature of the A Million Realities Fellowship is its nomination-based selection process.

Why Nominations Matter

A nomination serves as an independent endorsement of a candidate’s leadership and influence.

According to the Fellowship, a nomination is more than simply expressing interest—it acts as an important reference that demonstrates that others recognize the candidate’s contribution and leadership within the youth sector.

The Fellowship values nominations because they provide insight into a candidate’s standing that may not be fully captured in a self-written application.

Who Can Submit a Nomination?

There are two ways to enter the Fellowship selection process:

  1. Nominate another leader
  2. Nominate yourself

Although third-party nominations carry greater weight because they represent external recognition, self-nominated candidates have also successfully become A Million Realities Fellows in previous cohorts.

Anyone wishing to nominate another person is encouraged to inform the nominee beforehand and obtain their permission before sharing personal details.

Selection Timeline

The Fellowship follows a structured selection process over several months.

July – September 2026

  • Nominations open.

During this stage, nominations are submitted for prospective Fellows.

August – October 2026

  • Suitable nominees are invited to submit full Fellowship applications.

Not every nominee automatically progresses to the application stage. The Fellowship team reviews nominations before inviting selected candidates to continue.

27–28 October 2026

  • Interviews take place.

Candidates invited to interview will have an opportunity to discuss their leadership journey, impact, and aspirations.

November 2026 – March 2027

  • Successful Fellows complete onboarding.

During this period, selected participants prepare for the start of the Fellowship.

March 2027

  • The two-year Fellowship officially begins.

Fellowship Benefits

Although the announcement primarily focuses on the nomination process, becoming an A Million Realities Fellow offers significant professional and leadership development opportunities.

Participants can expect to benefit from:

  • Membership in a prestigious UK leadership network
  • Opportunities to collaborate with leaders across multiple sectors
  • Enhanced leadership development
  • Cross-sector networking
  • Professional learning and knowledge exchange
  • Greater visibility within the youth sector
  • Opportunities to contribute to improving youth outcomes across the UK
  • Participation in a two-year structured Fellowship programme
  • Long-term connections with other Fellows and sector leaders

The Fellowship is intended to strengthen participants’ capacity to create meaningful and lasting impact in their communities and organisations.

Live Online Q&A Sessions

Prospective nominees, applicants, and nominators are invited to attend online information sessions where members of the AMR team and current Fellows will explain the programme and answer questions.

The scheduled sessions are:

  • 24 July 2026
    • Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
  • 26 August 2026
    • Time: 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM

These sessions provide an excellent opportunity to learn more about the Fellowship experience, eligibility expectations, and application process before submitting a nomination.

Participants are encouraged to register in advance to receive joining details.

Who Should Consider Applying?

The Fellowship is ideal for individuals who:

  • Demonstrate leadership within the youth sector
  • Have a proven commitment to improving the lives of young people
  • Are creating measurable impact in their communities
  • Wish to strengthen their leadership capacity
  • Value collaboration and cross-sector partnerships
  • Are interested in joining a long-term network of changemakers

The programme welcomes both established and emerging leaders whose work contributes to positive youth outcomes.

How to Nominate a Leader

To participate in the Fellowship selection process:

  1. Complete the official nomination form.
  2. Choose whether you are nominating yourself or another individual.
  3. If nominating someone else, obtain their consent before submitting their information.
  4. Wait for the Fellowship team to review nominations.
  5. Suitable nominees may receive an invitation to submit a full application.

Contact Information

Applicants or nominators who have questions about the Fellowship can contact the A Million Realities team via:

Email: info@amillionrealities.com

Why You Should Apply

The A Million Realities Fellowship is an outstanding opportunity for leaders committed to improving opportunities for young people across the UK.

Rather than simply recognizing past achievements, the programme invests in future leadership by bringing together talented professionals from different sectors to collaborate, learn from one another, and build stronger solutions for young people.

Whether you work in education, mental health, housing, policy, philanthropy, youth development, or community leadership, the Fellowship provides an opportunity to expand your influence while becoming part of a powerful network dedicated to positive social change.

If you—or someone you know—is making a meaningful difference in the lives of young people, this Fellowship offers a valuable platform to grow, collaborate, and lead.

Click HERE to learn more and also apply for nomination.

Conclusion

The A Million Realities Fellowship 2027 seeks passionate leaders who are shaping better futures for young people throughout the United Kingdom. Beginning with a nomination process that recognizes leadership and community impact, the Fellowship offers participants the opportunity to join a two-year programme focused on leadership development, collaboration, and lasting change.

Prospective candidates are encouraged to submit nominations before the close of the nomination period, attend one of the online Q&A sessions to learn more about the programme, and prepare for the subsequent application and interview stages. For individuals dedicated to youth empowerment and systems change, the A Million Realities Fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their leadership journey while contributing to meaningful social impact.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post A Million Realities Fellowship 2027: Nominate Yourself or an Inspiring Youth Leader for a Two-Year Leadership Programme in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
